name: Zuppa Di Pane (Bread Soup) (Hl)
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the beans overnight in a large bowl with enough water to cover by 2 inches.
2. Drain the beans and rinse them under cool water.
3. Place the drained beans in a large pot with 3 quarts of water over high heat.
4. Add salt, 1/4 teaspoon pepper, and olive oil to the water; bring to a boil.
5. Lower the heat and cook the beans for about 1 hour or until tender.
6. Remove one-third of the beans and reserve them for garnish. Leave the remaining beans in their cooking water.
7. Remove the stems from swiss chard and finely julienne the leaves. Reserve the leaves.
8. Add the swiss chard stems, onion, carrots, celery, tomato paste, and remaining 1/4 teaspoon pepper to the cooked beans.
9. Bring to a boil and cover.
10. Reduce heat to medium-low and cook for about an hour or until vegetables and beans are very tender.
11. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
12. Slice the Italian bread and brush with remaining olive oil. Place on a baking sheet and toast until golden brown.
13. Remove the center rib from reserved swiss chard leaves and cut each leaf into eighths.
14. When the soup is ready, puree in a blender. It should have a thick, winter-soup consistency; if too thick, add some boiling water.
15. Mix in parsley, basil, reserved beans, and chopped swiss chard leaves. Adjust seasoning to taste.
16. Place 2 slices of toasted bread in each serving bowl. Ladle the soup on top and serve immediately.
Yield: 6 first course servings, or 4 as a main dish
Nutritional information: Calories: 557, Fat: 15 grams.
